text: This is a 76 year old patient who had two prior parathyroid surgery with an ectopic parathyroid lesion found in the chest and we approached this with VATS.
The lesion was identified in system UV scan, preoperative scan.
This is here the anatomical landmarks with the mammary vessels and the lungs and we identified

The parathyroid adenoma using intraoperative radio guided surgery with the gamma probe we identified the location of the parathyroid gland and circumferential dissection was performed with simple electrocautery around the lesion that was identified with the gamma probe.
additionally intraoperative PTH model was performed and after the circumferential dissection of the lesion and the removal of the parathyroid adenoma the PTH normalized
again here we are showing circumferential dissection with electrocautery after localization with the gamma probe
The patient treated the procedure well and remained eucalcemic for one year after her surgery.
